We are all naturally powerless in spiritual matters, blind, crippled, and withered; but complete provision is made for our healing if we pay attention to it. An angel went down and stirred the water; and whatever disease it was, this water healed it, but only the first person who stepped in received benefit. This teaches us to be careful that we do not miss an opportunity which may never return. The man had been unable to use his limbs for thirty-eight years. Should we, who perhaps for many years have scarcely known what it is to be sick for a day, complain of one wearisome night, when others, better than we, have scarcely known what it is to be well for a day? Christ singled this one out from the rest. Those who suffer long may comfort themselves that God keeps track of how long they have suffered. Observe that this man speaks of the unkindness of those around him without any bitter remarks. As we should be thankful, so we should be patient. Our Lord Jesus heals him even though he neither asked nor thought about it. Arise and walk. God’s command, Turn and live; Make you a new heart; does not more suppose power in us without the grace of God, his distinguishing grace, than this command supposed such power in the crippled man: it was by the power of Christ, and he must have all the glory. What a joyful surprise to the poor cripple to suddenly find himself so easy, so strong, so able to help himself! The proof of spiritual healing is our rising and walking. Has Christ healed our spiritual diseases, let us go wherever he sends us and take up whatever he lays upon us; and walk before him.

Those freed from the punishment of sin are in danger of returning to sin when the fear and restraint are over, unless Divine grace dries up the source. The misery that believers are made whole from warns us not to sin anymore, having felt the pain of sin. This is the voice of every circumstance, Go and do not sin again. Christ saw it necessary to give this warning; for it is common for people, when sick, to promise much; when newly recovered, to perform only a little; but after awhile to forget all. Christ spoke of the wrath that is yet to come, which is far worse than the many hours, or even years, of pain some wicked men suffer as a result of their unlawful indulgences. And if such afflictions are severe, how dreadful will be the everlasting punishment of the wicked!

The Divine power of the miracle proved Jesus to be the Son of God, and he declared that he worked with, and like unto his Father, as he saw fit. These ancient enemies of Christ understood him and became more violent, charging him not only with breaking the Sabbath but blasphemy, in calling God his own Father and making himself equal with God. But all things now, and at the final judgment, are committed to the Son, purposely that all men might honor the Son as they honor the Father; and whoever does not thus honor the Son, whatever he may think or claim, does not honor the Father who sent him.

Our Lord declared his authority and character as the Messiah. The time had come when the dead should hear his voice as the Son of God and live. Our Lord first refers to raising those who were dead in sin to newness of life by the power of the Spirit, and then to raising the dead in their graves. The office of Judge of all men can only be exercised by one who has all knowledge and almighty power. May we believe His testimony; thus our faith and hope will be in God, and we will not come into condemnation. And may His voice reach the hearts of those dead in sin; that they may do works fitting for repentance and prepare for the solemn day.

Our Lord returned to his declaration of complete agreement between the Father and the Son, and declared himself the Son of God. He had a higher testimony than that of John; his works bore witness to all he had said. But the Divine word had no abiding place in their hearts, as they refused to believe in Him whom the Father had sent, according to his ancient promises. The voice of God, accompanied by the power of the Holy Spirit, thus made effective for the conversion of sinners, still proclaims that this is the beloved Son, in whom the Father is well pleased. But when the hearts of men are full of pride, ambition, and love of the world, there is no room for the word of God to abide in them.

The Jews considered that eternal life was revealed to them in their Scriptures, and that they possessed it because they had the word of God in their hands. Jesus urged them to search those Scriptures with more diligence and attention. “You search the Scriptures,” and you do well to do so. They did indeed search the Scriptures, but it was with a view to their own glory. It is possible for men to be very studious in the letter of the Scriptures yet to be strangers to its power. Or, “Search the Scriptures,” and so it was spoken to them as an appeal. You profess to receive and believe the Scripture; let that be the judge. It is spoken to us as advising or commanding all Christians to search the Scriptures. Not only read them and hear them, but search them; which denotes diligence in examining and studying them. We must search the Scriptures for heaven as our great end; For in them you think you have eternal life. We must search the Scriptures for Christ, as the new and living Way that leads to this end. To this testimony Christ adds reproofs of their unbelief and wickedness, their neglect of him and his doctrine. Also he reproves their lack of love for God. But there is life with Jesus Christ for poor souls. Many who make a great profession of religion yet show they lack the love of God by their neglect of Christ and contempt of his commandments. It is the love of God in us, the love that is a living, active principle in the heart, which God will accept. They slighted and undervalued Christ because they admired and overvalued themselves. How can those believe who make the praise and applause of men their idol! When Christ and his followers are wondered at, how can those believe whose greatest ambition is to put on a good appearance?

Many trust in some form of doctrines or some parties who no more enter into the real meaning of those doctrines or the views of the people whose names they bear than the Jews did into those of Moses. Let us search and pray over the Scriptures, intent on finding eternal life; let us observe how Christ is the great subject of them, and daily apply to him for the life he bestows.
